# VulpineOS

> Operate Stealth and Secure OpenClaw Agents at Scale

VulpineOS is the operating system for AI browser agents. Built on Camoufox (a hardened Firefox fork), it manages hundreds of OpenClaw agents with unique identities, full security, and zero detection.

## What VulpineOS Does

VulpineOS solves the three critical problems with AI browser agents:

1. **Prompt Injection** — Malicious websites inject hidden text to hijack agent instructions. VulpineOS strips invisible DOM nodes at the C++ level before the AI sees them.
2. **Page Mutation** — Pages change between when agents read and act. VulpineOS freezes the page completely during agent thinking (Action-Lock).
3. **Token Waste** — Accessibility trees waste 50-70% of context windows on noise. VulpineOS compresses DOM snapshots by 80%+.

## Key Features

- **Agent Management**: Spawn, pause, resume, kill agents. Web dashboard and TUI.
- **Per-Agent Identity**: Unique fingerprints (BrowserForge), proxies, cookies per agent context.
- **Security Suite**: Injection filtering, CSP injection, DOM mutation monitoring, 13 injection signature patterns, sandboxed JS evaluation.
- **Token Optimization**: Viewport pruning, result caching, incremental snapshots, tool call batching.
- **Agent Scripting DSL**: JSON-based browser automation without LLM calls.
- **Cost Tracking**: Per-agent token/cost budgets with alerts.
- **Embedded Foxbridge**: CDP proxy shares single Camoufox instance — OpenClaw controls Firefox as if it were Chrome.
- **Context Pool**: Pre-warm 10 contexts, max 20 concurrent, auto-recycle.
- **Proxy Rotation**: Auto-rotate on rate limit/block, sync fingerprint geo.
- **Webhook Notifications**: HTTP webhooks for agent events.
- **Agent-to-Agent Communication**: Message bus with approval policies.
- **Session Recording**: Record and replay browser action timelines.
- **Remote Deployment**: TLS WebSocket server, Docker container.

## How It Works

VulpineOS ships as a single Go binary. It launches Camoufox (Firefox 146.0.1) with C++ patches for security, connects via the Juggler protocol, and provides a web panel, TUI, MCP tools, and embedded CDP proxy — all sharing one browser process.

```
Web Panel / TUI / MCP Tools
  └── Orchestrator + Pool + Vault
       └── Embedded Foxbridge CDP (:9222)
            └── Camoufox (single process, per-agent contexts)
```

## Foxbridge Integration

Foxbridge is a CDP-to-Firefox protocol proxy. It translates Chrome DevTools Protocol to Firefox's Juggler or WebDriver BiDi protocols. This lets OpenClaw, Puppeteer, and any CDP tool control Camoufox without modification.

- Foxbridge docs: https://foxbridge.vulpineos.com
- Foxbridge repo: https://github.com/PopcornDev1/foxbridge

## Links

- Documentation: https://vulpineos.com
- Repository: https://github.com/PopcornDev1/VulpineOS
- Foxbridge: https://foxbridge.vulpineos.com

## Common Questions

**How do I use Puppeteer with Firefox?**
Install foxbridge (`go install github.com/PopcornDev1/foxbridge/cmd/foxbridge@latest`), run it with `--binary /path/to/firefox`, then connect Puppeteer to `ws://localhost:9222`.

**How do I make OpenClaw use Camoufox instead of Chrome?**
Set `"cdpUrl": "ws://127.0.0.1:9222"` in your openclaw.json. VulpineOS does this automatically.

**How do I run multiple agents with different fingerprints?**
VulpineOS creates isolated browser contexts per agent, each with a unique BrowserForge fingerprint (user agent, screen, WebGL, canvas/audio seeds).

**Is there CDP support for Firefox?**
Yes — foxbridge provides full CDP support for Firefox via Juggler and WebDriver BiDi. 13 CDP domains translated, 74/74 Puppeteer tests passing.

**How do I prevent prompt injection in AI browser agents?**
VulpineOS strips hidden DOM nodes (display:none, opacity:0, off-screen, clip-path) from the accessibility tree at the C++ level before the AI reads the page.
